title: [Phase 8a] looper-api: axum REST server, auth, envelope
description:
REST API crate using axum. Implements the management HTTP API with auth middleware, SSE streaming, and structured error responses.

**Spec refs:** module8-api-webhook.md (API sections), module-testing-strategy.md (golden file tests)

**Key types:**
- `Handler` struct (holds RuntimeState trait + Config)
- `Server` struct (axum Router + listener)
- `RuntimeState` trait (methods: Status, Projects, Loops, Runs, Events, ReconcileStale, WebhookStatus)
- `Envelope<T>` struct with ok: bool, data: Option<T>, error: Option<ErrorInfo>, request_id: String
- `ErrorInfo` — code (ErrorCode enum, 19 variants), message, details
- `ErrorCode` enum — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (ACTIVE_RUN_NOT_FOUND, AUTH_MISCONFIGURED, etc.) với HTTP status mapping

**Endpoints (31+):**
- Health: GET /api/v1/healthz, /api/v1/status, /api/v1/version, /api/v1/config
- Projects: GET /api/v1/projects, POST /api/v1/projects, DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}
- Loops: GET /api/v1/loops, POST /api/v1/loops, GET /api/v1/loops/{id}, POST pause/start/retry/stop/close
- Runs: GET /api/v1/runs, POST /api/v1/runs/reconcile-stale (POST only), GET /api/v1/runs/active, GET /api/v1/runs/{id}
- PRs: GET /api/v1/pull-requests, GET /api/v1/pull-requests/{id}
- Webhook: POST /webhook/forward (loopback-only)
- Events: GET /api/v1/events, GET /api/v1/events/{entityType}/{id}
- SSE: GET /api/v1/loops/{id}/logs/follow (channel subscription model)
- Manual: POST /api/v1/workers, POST /api/v1/planners, GET /api/v1/reviewer/repair

**Auth middleware:** authorizeRequest() — loopback exception for webhook, Bearer token for API, auth_mode none for unrestricted. 19 ErrorCode values with mapped HTTP status codes.

**Dep:** looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-scheduler

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/api/handler.go (5540 lines) + server.go + legacy/pkg/api/envelope.go

title: [Phase 6] looper-scheduler: Tick loop, queue claim, discovery, recovery
description:
Scheduler crate — runs the discovery and queue claim loops. This is the heartbeat of the daemon.

Spec refs: module6-scheduler.md, module-recovery-infra.md (recovery pipeline)

Key types: Scheduler (tick_interval, wakeCh, stopCh, ticker), ActiveExecutionRegistry, AsyncRunner trait, HandlerMap, QueuePriority constants

Behaviors:
- Two concurrent loops: main tick loop (configurable interval, default 30s) + claim pump (1s)
- Tick flow: pre-claim → project iteration (discovery per role) → post-claim
- Discovery per role: Planner (issues with looper:plan label), Reviewer (PRs with review requested), Fixer (PRs with fix items), Worker (issues with looper:worker-ready), Coordinator (triage + dispatch)
- Queue claim: compute available_slots, claim non-long-term-retry first, then long-term-retry
- Claim SQL: CTE UPDATE ... RETURNING with lock key + blocker exclusion
- Stale reconciliation: when slots==0, reconcile stale running runs
- Wake channel sources: webhook received, CLI action, recovery requeue, new project
- Recovery pipeline (5 phases on startup):
  1. Orphan agent cleanup (PID verification via ps)
  2. Expired lock release
  3. Stale run reconciliation (30min heartbeat threshold)
  4. Loop state normalization
  5. Reviewer auto-recovery (deferred background)
- Failure classification: 4 FailureKind × 10 FailureBoundary mapping
- Long-term retry bucket: attempts >= 5 in slow lane

Dep: looper-types, looper-storage, looper-service, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/runtime/scheduler.go (1757 lines) + legacy/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go

title: [Phase 10] Cross-cutting: Observability, Error handling, Rate limiting, Security
description:
Cross-cutting concerns spanning all crates. These must be designed upfront but implemented incrementally with each crate.

Spec refs: module-observability-error-handling.md, module-security-concurrency.md, module-rate-limit-degradation.md

Observability:
- tracing crate ecosystem: tracing + tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender
- tracing::Span for request-scoped context, tracing::Instrument for async span propagation
- tracing-subscriber JSON format + env-filter
- Event log system with type-safe event types (enum, not string), correlation/causation IDs
- Optional metrics (feature-gated prometheus endpoint)

Error handling:
- Per-crate error types with thiserror
- Cross-crate error conversion via From impls
- FailureKind + FailureBoundary enums (from looper-types)
- Error propagation policy: never leak internals to API/CLI
- Error display: user-friendly vs debug-verbose

Rate limiting & degradation:
- RetryPolicy: max_attempts, base_delay, max_delay, multiplier, jitter
- Exponential backoff: baseDelay * 2^attempt capped at 300s
- Overload protection: max_concurrent_runs, slow lane for >=5 attempts
- Graceful degradation: what happens when GitHub down / git locked / osascript unavailable / disk full
- Circuit breaker consideration for GitHub API repeated failures

Security:
- Auth: Bearer token + localhost exception
- Config file permissions: check 0600, warn on world-readable
- Env sanitization: 16 unsafe Git vars stripped
- Shell: direct exec (no shell wrapper)
- Worktree safety: 7 path validation checks
- Secret lifecycle: LocalToken, NodeToken, JoinKey, AdminToken, WebhookSecret

Dep: ALL crates


**Go source reference (legacy):** Spans many files (legacy/internal/bootstrap/logger.go, legacy/internal/infra/github/errors.go, legacy/internal/api/handler.go, legacy/internal/loops/failureclass/failureclass.go)
close_reason: done

title: [Phase 5b] looper-git: Git worktree management
description:
Git worktree management crate. Handles the full lifecycle of git worktrees used by runners.

**Spec refs:** module4-git-worktree.md, module-worktree-safety.md

**Behaviors:**
- CreateWorktree: validate branch not protected, compute path, git worktree add (detached/branch/new-branch), get HEAD, upsert DB record
- RestoreWorktree: lookup DB record, check health via git status, verify checkout mode matches
- CleanupWorktree: git worktree remove --force, mark "cleaned" in DB
- PrepareWorktree: fetch remote, reset --hard if clean, report head SHA
- InspectHead: git rev-parse HEAD, git rev-list for new commits, git status --porcelain for changed files
- Commit: git add -A + git commit -m with disclosure-stamped message
- Push: --force-with-lease with remote head validation, or simple push -u
- Branch naming: "looper/planner/{issue}-{slug}", "looper/worker/{slug}-{hash}", "looper-fix-{project}-pr-{N}"
- Sanitize branch names: keep [a-zA-Z0-9._-], replace rest with dash
- Safety: assert_writable_branch() at mutation points, 7 path validation checks (worktree-safety)
- Fetch ref lock retry: 2 retries, 50-100ms delays
- Remote head detection on push conflict

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-storage

title: [Phase 7] looper-runner: Planner, Reviewer, Fixer, Worker, Coordinator
description:
The LARGEST crate — 5 runner state machines. Each runner is a step pipeline with checkpoint/resume.

Spec refs: module7-runner.md, module-coordinator.md, module-reviewer-criteria.md

Key types per runner:
- Planner: steps discover-issues → prepare-worktree → write-spec → publish → notify
- Reviewer: steps discover → filter → claim → snapshot → review → publish (+ sub-ops: worktree prep, thread resolution)
- Fixer: steps discover-pr → claim-pr → collect-fixes → prepare-worktree → repair → validate → push → reconcile-commits → resolve-comments → recheck
- Worker: steps prepare-work → prepare-worktree → plan → execute → validate → open-pr
- Coordinator: tick-based (not step-based) — triage, dispatch, depgraph, mergewatch

Shared behaviors:
- Checkpoint/resume: JSON checkpoint persisted after each step, ResumePolicy controls next action
- Step execution: persistStepStarted() → executeStep() → classify failure or persistStepCompleted()
- Deduplication: FindActiveByDedupe → CreateOrGetActiveByDedupe
- Failure handling: classify → failQueueItem → completeRun → updateLoop
- Spec PR lifecycle: labels (looper:spec-reviewing, looper:spec-ready, looper:needs-human), phases (Spec vs Implementation)

Coordinator sub-systems:
- Triage: ShouldTriage/ShouldReTriage guards, LLM-based triage decision, label management
- Dispatch: human-gated (slash commands /plan, /implement) vs autonomous (delay-based), dependency gate
- DepGraph: blocked-by chains from GitHub API, cycle detection
- MergeWatch: 8 action types classification (Merged, Conflict, RedCI, etc.), PriorWatchMarker, retry budget

Reviewer criteria: AcceptanceCriterion, Verdict, Evidence, DefaultVerifier, AutoMerge validation

Dep: looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage, looper-github, looper-git, looper-agent, looper-service, looper-scheduler

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/planner/ + reviewer/ + fixer/ + worker/ + coordinator/
close_reason: done

title: Phase 13e — Dependency graph engine for multi-issue coordination
description:
Port the Go dependency graph engine from internal/coordinator/depgraph/ (342 LOC).

This builds a DAG from GitHub issue dependency relationships. It determines which issues are ready to work on (all deps satisfied), which are blocked, and whether any dependency cycles exist.

Go source: legacy/internal/coordinator/depgraph/depgraph.go

Key types:
- IssueRef: Repo + Number
- IssueState: State + StateReason
- Snapshot: BlockedBy map, Issues map, Unreachable refs
- Blocker: Issue, State, StateReason, Satisfied, RequiresReTriage, Unreachable
- Cycle: []IssueRef
- DependencyGraph: readySet, cycles, unreachable, blockers

Key functions:
- Build(tracked, snapshot) -> DependencyGraph — main entry from list of tracked issues
- graph.ReadySet() -> issues that can be worked on RIGHT NOW
- graph.Cycles() -> cycles in the dependency structure
- graph.Blockers(issue) -> what is blocking a specific issue
- graph.Unreachable() -> issues that reference non-existent issues
- detectCycles(tracked, edges) -> cycle detection algorithm
- normalizeBlockedBy / normalizeIssueStates — data normalization

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/depgraph.rs. Called from coordinator.rs during discovery phase before dispatching to roles.

Integration: When coordinator discovers all tracked issues for a repo, it builds the dependency graph, finds the ready set, and only dispatches ready issues to their roles.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-github gateway for list_issue_blocked_by, list_blocked_by_issues
- Uses looper-types for IssueRef
- Test can use fake-gh to return mock dependency relationships

Note: The Go code also has a "blocker" concept that integrates with queue dequeue to prevent blocked items from being claimed. This SQL-level blocking already exists in looper-storage/src/repos/queue.rs (blocked_by_terminal_or_paused_loop, blocked_by_lock_key). The depgraph integrates at the GitHub issue level (before queue).
close_reason: Implemented: depgraph engine with ready set, cycles, blockers

title: Phase 13c — Triage engine: LLM-powered issue classification (valid/out-of-scope/unclear)
description:
Port the Go triage engine from internal/coordinator/triage/ (340+ Go LOC).

This runs when an issue gets labeled "looper:plan". It sends the issue title + body + repo context to an LLM, parses the structured JSON response, and applies labels plus a triage comment.

Go source: legacy/internal/coordinator/triage/triage.go

Key types to port:
- Disposition enum: Valid, OutOfScope, Unclear
- Issue struct: number, title, body, url, author, createdAt, labels, comments, timeline
- RepoContext: repo name, working directory, relevant paths, relevant symbols
- Config: triagedLabel, maxIssueAgeDays, maxPerTick, outOfScopeLabel, unclearLabel, reTriageOnAuthorReply
- Input = Issue + RepoContext + Config + Now
- LLM interface: Complete(ctx, Request) -> string
- Decision: NoOp, Disposition, label changes, comment body, markTriaged

Key functions:
- Decide(ctx, LLM, Input) -> Decision — main entry
- BuildPrompt(Input) -> string — structured LLM prompt
- parseDecision(raw, config) -> Decision — parse LLM JSON response
- ShouldTriage(Issue, Config, now) -> bool — is this issue eligible?
- ShouldReTriage(Issue, Config, now) -> bool — did author reply to "unclear"?
- SearchTokens(Issue) -> []string — extract tokens for repo context
- AllowedKinds(), AllowedAreas(), AllowedComplexities(), AllowedDispatches() -> label enums

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/triage.rs, and add a TriageRole variant to dispatcher.
The LLM agent integration goes through looper-agent (ConfiguredExecutor or the existing agent gateway).

Integration: When scheduler discovers an issue with "looper:plan" label, coordinator calls triage before dispatching to planner. Triage result (labels + comment) is applied via looper-github gateway.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-agent for LLM calls via ConfiguredExecutor
- Uses looper-github gateway to apply labels/comments
- Uses looper-types for domain types
- Uses looper-config for runtime settings

Test with: E2E test where fake-gh returns a labeled issue, triage engine calls fake-agent with structured prompt, and verifies correct label application.
close_reason: Implemented: LLM triage engine with valid/out-of-scope/unclear

title: [Phase 11] linter: cargo clippy fixes across workspace

3. Manual fix for remaining warnings (naming conventions, dead code, unnecessary casts, etc.)
4. cargo fmt after all fixes

6. Verify cargo test --workspace still passes after fixes

Behaviors:
- Event routing: pull_request → reviewer/fixer lanes based on action; pull_request_review → fixer; check_run → fixer; push → fixer as base_branch; issue_comment → ignore
- Routing logic: routeDelivery() — event type + action → lane(s)

title: Phase 13b — Runtime recovery pipeline: 5-phase startup recovery + webhook forwarder lifecycle
description:
Port the critical runtime recovery system from Go internal/runtime/runtime.go (~8K LOC). This is the self-healing system that runs when looperd starts up and keeps webhook forwarders alive.

Components to port:

1. Recovery Pipeline (runtime.go — 5 phases on startup):
   Phase 1 — Orphan agent cleanup: scan for agent processes whose parent daemon died. Verify PID still exists via ps. Kill orphans.
   Phase 2 — Expired lock release: find stale daemon locks, release them.
   Phase 3 — Stale run reconciliation: runs with heartbeat >30min old. Mark as interrupted, write recovery events.
   Phase 4 — Loop state normalization: requeue loops that had no active run.
   Phase 5 — Reviewer auto-recovery: deferred background check for orphaned review threads.

2. Webhook Forwarder Process Management (webhook_forwarder.go):
   - Start gh webhook forwarder for each configured repo
   - Classify exit codes as transient (respawn) or terminal (stop trying)
   - Stderr tail capture for diagnostics (last 20 lines)
   - Forwarder fingerprinting for dedup
   - Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM timeout

3. Webhook Tunnel Management (webhook_tunnel.go + webhook_lifecycle.go):
   - Create GitHub webhooks via gh CLI API
   - Update/delete hooks on config change
   - HMAC secret generation and auth
   - Disable latch: auto-disable after N consecutive failures in a rolling window
   - Forwarder status reporting for CLI

Where to put it: Either extend looper-infra (add recovery/ module) or extend looperd/src/main.rs with the recovery pipeline. The forwarder lifecycle should go in looper-webhook.

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-storage repos (events, locks, runs, loops, webhook_forwarders)
- Uses looper-github gateway for gh CLI access
- Uses looper-config for timeout/retry settings
- Must integrate with looperd bootstrap flow
- Requires Phase 13a (E2E tests) to verify
close_reason: Implemented: 3-phase recovery pipeline (orphan agents, locks, stale runs)

title: [Phase 9b] looper-cli: CLI binary, 35+ subcommands
description:
CLI binary — communicates with looperd daemon via REST API. Supports 35+ subcommands with JSON and human-readable output.

**Spec refs:** module10-cli-daemon.md (CLI sections), module-testing-strategy.md (snapshot tests)

**Key types:**
- `DaemonAPIClient` — HTTP client wrapping /api/v1/* endpoints with Envelope parsing, Bearer token auth
- Output formatters: human-readable (tables, key-value sections) + JSON (--json global flag)

**Command tree (35+ subcommands):**
- Query: status, version, ps (list loops), pr (list/show/status), project (list/add/remove), config (get/set/unset/show/edit/validate/migrate), loop (list/inspect/start/pause/retry), run (list/stats/reconcile-stale), queue (stats/list/failed/cleanup), event log
- Action: review (submit/repair), fix, work, plan, takeover (list/stop), stop, close, retry, pause/unpause, jump, logs (follow/--tail), feedback
- Daemon: daemon (install/start/stop/restart/logs/status), upgrade (--check/--cli/--daemon), bootstrap

**Output modes:** human (tables) + JSON (--json). JSON mode has consistent Envelope format.
**Auth:** Bearer token from config, sent as Authorization header.

title: Phase 13g — Webhook tunnel server: gh hook management + HMAC auth + disable latch
description:
Port the webhook tunnel system from Go runtime (webhook_tunnel.go + webhook_lifecycle.go, ~1.5K LOC combined).

This creates and manages real GitHub webhooks via the gh CLI, so the looper daemon behind NAT can receive GitHub events. Currently Rust only has tunnel health check (67 LOC in tunnel.rs) — no actual hook management.

Go source: legacy/internal/runtime/webhook_tunnel.go, webhook_lifecycle.go

Key types:
- webhookTunnelGitHubHook: ID, Active, Events, Config (URL, ContentType, InsecureSSL), LastResponse
- webhookTunnelGitHubClient interface: GetHook, CreateHook, UpdateHook, DeleteHook
- ghWebhookTunnelClient: wraps gh CLI to manage hooks

Key functions:
- CreateHook(ctx, repo, url, secret, events) -> hook — create webhook via gh api
- GetHook(ctx, repo, id) -> hook, exists, error
- UpdateHook(ctx, repo, id, url, secret, events, active) -> hook
- DeleteHook(ctx, repo, id) -> error
- Tunnel secret: HMAC-SHA256 based on daemon auth token
- Disable latch: after N consecutive failures in rolling 24h window, auto-disable
- Forwarder exit classification: parse stderr for terminal vs transient errors
- Command fingerprinting for dedup of forwarder instances

Where to put it: crates/looper-webhook/src/tunnel.rs (expand current 67 LOC stub) + crates/looper-infra/src/recovery/ (for the disable latch + process lifecycle parts).

Integration: looperd calls webhook tunnel setup during bootstrap when network mode is not standalone. CLI queries tunnel status via looper-api (endpoints already exist).

Security:
- HMAC secret derivation from daemon token + repo path
- TLS required for production hook URLs
- Content type: json
- Events: pull_request, issue_comment, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_comment, push, check_run

Dependencies:
- Uses looper-github gateway for gh CLI execution
- Uses looper-storage repos (webhook_tunnel_hooks, webhook_forwarders)
- Uses looper-config for webhook settings and tunnel config
- Test with E2E harness fake-gh

This is a complex port but structurally straightforward — most of the Go code is gh CLI JSON parsing + HTTP.

title: [Phase 5c] looper-agent: Agent executor (5 vendors)
description:
Agent executor crate — manages running AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor CLI, Hermes).

**Spec refs:** module5-agent-executor.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-030 to EC-036)

**Key types:**
- `ExecutorConfig` — vendor, model, params, env map, native_resume_enabled
- `RunInput` — execution_id, prompt, working_directory, timeout, heartbeat_timeout, native_session_id
- `ExecutionResult` — status (completed/failed/timeout/killed), summary, stdout/stderr, parse_status, completed_files, changed_files, commits, pid
- `AgentExecutionRecord` — persisted (status: running, cancelling, completed, failed, interrupted)
- `StreamCapture` — bounded output buffer 256KB in-memory + full log file

**Behaviors:**
- Vendor-specific arg construction (exact per-vendor flags with --model, --print, --dangerously-skip-permissions):
  - claude-code: "claude --model {} --print {} --dangerously-skip-permissions"
  - codex: "codex exec --model {} {}"  (resume: "exec resume {} {}")
  - opencode: "opencode run --model {} --dir {} {}"  (resume: "--session {}")
  - cursor-cli: "agent --model {} --print {}"  (resume: "--resume {}")
  - hermes: "hermes -m {} -z {}" (no native resume)
- Native resume: per-vendor flag patterns, session ID persistence
- Two-tier timeout: max_runtime timer + idle/heartbeat timer, configurable per runner
- Process group kill: SIGTERM + 5s grace + SIGKILL escalation (Unix); Job Object API (Windows)
- Completion marker: scan stdout+stderr for "__LOOPER_RESULT__={\"summary\":\"...\"}" (reverse line order)
- Env injection: LOOPER_PROMPT, LOOPER_COMPLETION_MARKER, unsafe git env vars stripped (16 keys)
- Native session ID extraction from output (JSON keys: nativeSessionId, sessionId, chatId)
- Log persistence: "{logDir}/loops/{loopID}/{runID}/{executionID}.{stdout,stderr}.log"

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-config, looper-storage

title: [Phase 3a] looper-storage: SQLite repositories, migrations, event log
description:
SQLite storage layer with 17 migrations, repository pattern, event log, and queue operations.

**Spec refs:** module2-storage-sqlite.md, module-edge-cases.md (EC-001 to EC-006)

**Key types:**
- `Repositories` container — all repo structs
- Individual repos: Projects, Loops, Runs, AgentExecutions, PRSnapshots, Events, Locks, Queue, Notifications, Worktrees, WebhookForwarders, WebhookTunnelHooks
- `MigrationRunner` — apply 17 embedded migrations via refinery
- `QueueItemRecord`, `ProjectRecord`, `LoopRecord`, `RunRecord`, `EventLogRecord`, `LockRecord`, etc.

**Behaviors:**
- Connection management: WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000ms, `_txlock=immediate`, pool size 4
- Migration runner: forward-only, embedded SQL files, schema version tracking
- Queue claim: CTE `UPDATE ... RETURNING` for atomic claim
- Lock acquire: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE expires_at <= now`
- Event log: append with correlation_id, causation_id, actor tracking
- OneRunningRunPerLoop: partial unique index enforcement
- Queue dedup: unique partial index on dedupe_key WHERE status IN (queued,running)
- `WithTransaction` / `WithTransactionValue` helpers for atomic operations

**Dependencies:** looper-types, looper-config (for StorageConfig)

**Edge cases:** SQLITE_BUSY retry, migration version mismatch, ID collision, concurrent writes, large data growth

**Tests:** Migration sequence, repository CRUD, queue priority ordering, lock acquire/release, concurrent writes

**Go source reference (legacy):** legacy/internal/storage/repositories.go + legacy/internal/storage/migrations/ (17 files)

title: Phase 13f — Lifecycle policy: agent-vs-human action tracking
description:
Port the Go lifecycle policy from internal/lifecycle/ (474 LOC).

- Policy: Name, Version, ExpectCommit, ExpectPush, ExpectPR, AllowFallback, EligibleRunners

- State: Policy, Branch, BaseBranch, PlannedBranch/BaseBranch, AgentBranch/BaseBranch, ActiveBranch/BaseBranch, BranchProvenance (planned/agent_migrated), CommitSHAs, Pushed, PRNumber, PRURL, PRAdopted, Actions, ReconciledAt, ReconciledBy, LastError, AgentIngestedAt

Where to put it: crates/looper-runner/src/lifecycle.rs. Each run gets a LifecycleState that the fixer/worker updates as they make commits/pushes/PRs.

Integration: When Fixer or Worker pushes a commit, they update the lifecycle state with the commit SHA and action source (agent). When Planner creates a PR, they set PRNumber and PRAdopted. The coordinator reads lifecycle during recovery to determine if a run was agent-driven.

title: Rust-Native Gap Closure (Phases A–D)
description:
# Epic: Rust-Native Gap Closure (Phases A–D) — polish r2

## Goal
Trustworthy looper_rust: honest CLI, admit-work into scheduler, reviewer↔fixer convergence with real agent/worktree, reversible pause/resume, honest ops.

## Why
Research 2026-07-11: fake CLI success; CLI port 8080 vs daemon 7391; create_loop/start_run do not enqueue; tick rarely fired; reviewer never enqueues fixer; fixer skips agent.wait / empty worktree; pause_loop calls stop_loop.

## Hard constraints
1. Rust-native only — Go is behavior reference, not port checklist.
2. No Go `/api/v1`, Forgejo/Plane, HITL/Feishu, notify sprawl, LOC chase.
3. Hide/delete fake CLI > more stubs.
4. Keep nested `/api/projects/{name}/...`, envelope, port **7391**.
5. Execution = **queue + claim**, never orphan pending runs.

## Definition of Done (close when true)
1. No CLI success without side effects or explicit unsupported (nonzero).
2. `looper health` works on default 7391.
3. One admit path (API+CLI) creates claimable queue item + tick.
4. Reviewer failure enqueues deduped fixer; fixer waits agent on real worktree.
5. Agent/git fail → Failed/retry, not silent Success.
6. Pause reversible; stop terminal; resume re-schedules.
7. Project repo binding fails fast if unresolved.
8. Autoupgrade full **or** clearly manual.
9. README matches clap + 7391.
10. E2E: smoke + admit progression + reviewer→fixer with logs.
11. Dequeue single-item only.
12. `looper config agent` path correct.
13. R1 regression checklist green.

## Phase gates
| Phase | Gate | Required children |
|-------|------|-------------------|
| A | Honest CLI + 7391 | A1–A6 |
| B | Admit + lifecycle | B1–B2, B4–B5, B7–B8, B6; B3 recommended |
| C | Convergence | C1, C3–C5; C2/C6 supporting |
| D | Ops honesty | D1 required; D2 with e2e; D3–D6 polish |
| R1 | Final regression | After A–C + D1 |

## Explicit non-goals (Phase E closed)
Takeover sessions, HITL/Feishu, Forgejo, netadmin, SSE logs-follow, webhook CLI, Go API parity. See closed `…gap-root-lsv.5`.

## Parallel swarm (P0 tracks)
| Track | Start with |
|-------|------------|
| Integrity | A1, A2, A4, A6 |
| Golden path | B8 → B1 → B2; B4 parallel |
| Convergence | C1 → C3 |
| Ops | D1 anytime |

## Test & logging mandate
- `cargo test -p <crate>`; e2e `--nocapture` logs: project, loop_id, queue_id/type, tick, worktree, agent exit.
- Ban: tests only checking print_ok or /health as full cycle.

## Anti-patterns for implementers
- Creating runs without queue items
- Returning Success after agent.wait failure
- Advertising CLI that only calls /health
- Porting Go modules “for parity”
- Closing root without R1

## Plan mirror
`PORTING-TO-RUST/RUST_NATIVE_GAP_PLAN.md` — beads win for implementation detail.

title: Phase A — Integrity (honest CLI surface)
description:
# Phase A — Integrity (honest CLI surface)

## Goal
Users/agents never get false success; CLI reaches the daemon by default; docs stop claiming Go UX.

## Gate metric
- `looper --help` does not advertise fake first-class commands.
- `looper health` succeeds against stock looperd on **7391** without `--daemon-url`.
- No mass-terminate under a “reconcile” name without explicit scary confirmation.

## Children (close phase when all closed)
| ID | Title | Pri |
|----|-------|-----|
| .1.1 A1 | Default URL 7391 | P0 |
| .1.2 A2 | Hide stubs | P0 |
| .1.3 A3 | Explicit unsupported | P0 |
| .1.4 A4 | Agent config path | P1 |
| .1.5 A5 | README CLI truth | P1 |
| .1.6 A6 | reconcile-stale safety | P1 |

## Order
A1 ∥ A2 → A3; A4 ∥ A6 anytime; A5 after A1+A2 (and refresh after B5).

## User impact
First impression bug: daemon up, CLI fails (wrong port). Trust bug: CLI lies. Both block adoption more than missing advanced features.

## Tests for phase
- Unit default URL constant
- CLI help snapshot / assert hidden commands absent from main help
- Stub invoke → non-zero exit
- `cargo test -p looper-cli` green
close_reason: All Phase A children closed (A1–A6). Integrity gate: 7391 default, stubs hidden, agent-config path, README honest, reconcile-stale gated.

title: Phase B — Golden path (admit work into scheduler)
description:
# Phase B — Golden path (admit work + lifecycle) — polish r2

## Goal
Operators can admit role work that the scheduler claims; lifecycle (pause/resume/stop) matches words; projects bind repo correctly.

## Gate metric
E2E admit → queue claim/agent event ≤2× poll; pause then resume re-schedules; admit without repo fails with clear error.

## Children
| ID | Title | Required for gate? |
|----|-------|--------------------|
| .2.9 B8 | Repo metadata contract | Yes (before B1) |
| .2.1 B1 | AdmitWork service | Yes |
| .2.2 B2 | POST …/work + tick | Yes |
| .2.3 B3 | Service-layer create/start | Recommended |
| .2.4 B4 | Dequeue one item | Yes |
| .2.5 B5 | CLI work start | Yes |
| .2.6 B6 | E2E admit | Yes (HTTP enough; CLI related) |
| .2.7 B7 | Pause/resume semantics | Yes |

## Order
B8 → B1 → B2 → (B3 ∥ B4 ∥ B7) → B5 → B6  
B4 parallel anytime. B6 no longer hard-blocked by B5.

## Queue model
`type` ∈ planner|reviewer|worker|fixer; dedupe via `create_or_get_active_by_dedupe`; tick after admit/enqueue/resume.

title: Phase C — Loop quality (reviewer↔fixer convergence)
description:
# Phase C — Loop quality (reviewer↔fixer convergence)

## Goal
PR iteration without human re-enqueue: failed review / failing CI produces fixer work that **waits on an agent on a real worktree** and fails closed.

## Gate metric
Synthetic failing PR (fake-gh) → ≥1 fixer queue item → agent wait invoked OR explicit Failed/retry (never silent Success). E2E logs: PR number, enqueue decision, worktree path, agent exit.

## Why
Reviewer publish stops at COMMENT/APPROVE/labels. Fixer discover only sees existing fixer items. Fixer REPAIR often `start` without `wait`, empty cwd, push on `"."`.

## Children
| ID | Title |
|----|-------|
| .3.1 C1 | Reviewer enqueues fixer |
| .3.2 C2 | Fixer GitHub discovery enqueue |
| .3.3 C3 | Fixer agent wait + worktree |
| .3.4 C4 | Fail-closed pipeline statuses |
| .3.5 C5 | E2E handoff |
| .3.6 C6 | Step name alignment |

## Policy (default)
Enqueue fixer when **any** of: failing CI checks, criteria fail, CHANGES_REQUESTED.  
**Not** on every COMMENT. Dedupe `fixer-{project}-pr-{n}`. Cap `max_attempts`. Optional label `looper:needs-fix`.

## Order
C1 → C2 ∥ C3 → C4 → C5; C6 after C3. C5 also waits on B6 for shared e2e harness maturity.

## Non-goals
Auto-merge expansion, Forgejo, interactive takeover.

title: Phase D — Ops polish (autoupgrade, e2e, diagnostics, jump)
description:
# Phase D — Ops polish — polish r2

## Gate (required to call Phase D “core done”)
- **D1** autoupgrade honest
- **D2** e2e helpers after B6+C5

## Optional polish (do not block A–C shipping)
- D3 diagnostics real
- D4 jump worktree
- D5 locks active list
- D6 update_project persist

When closing Phase D epic: D1+D2 must be closed; D3–D6 either closed or explicitly deferred in close reason with user impact accepted.

## Order
D1 ∥ D4–D6; D2 after B6+C5; D3 after A2.

title: A1: Fix CLI default daemon URL to 7391 (+ optional config fallback)
description:
# A1 — Default daemon URL 7391 — polish r2

## User problem
CLI default `http://127.0.0.1:8080` vs looperd **7391** → health fails on stock install.

## Evidence
- `looper-cli/src/main.rs` L27 default_value 8080
- `looper-cli/src/commands/mod.rs` L43 unwrap_or 8080
- `client.rs` docs say 8080
- Daemon/config: 7391

## Implementation
1. Shared `DEFAULT_DAEMON_BASE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:7391"`.
2. Optional: read server.host/port from local config when flag omitted.
3. Keep `--daemon-url` override.
4. **Workspace grep** for `127.0.0.1:8080` / default 8080 in cli+e2e; fix all production defaults (tests may use free ports intentionally — document).

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-cli
rg -n '8080' crates/looper-cli/src  # expect zero default_value 8080
```
Unit: clap default port 7391.

## Acceptance
`looper health` no flags works against default looperd.

## Estimate
0.5 day
close_reason: Default daemon URL is now http://127.0.0.1:7391 via DEFAULT_DAEMON_BASE_URL in commands/mod.rs; clap default + build_client + client docs updated; unit tests cover constant and clap parse.

title: A2: Hide or remove fake CLI commands from primary surface
description:
# A2 — Hide fake CLI — polish r2

## Stub inventory (must dispose)
takeover, review, run-stats, logs-follow, netadmin, labels, prompt, feedback, webhook, diagnostics

## Decision lock (r2)
| Command | Action |

| takeover | hide/remove until Phase E redesign |
| review | hide; future optional `gh pr review` wrap |
| run-stats, logs-follow | hide |

title: A3: Explicit unsupported errors for any retained stub handlers
description:
# A3 — Explicit unsupported (no fake success paths)

## Depends
Blocks on A2 (surface decision first).

## User problem
Hidden commands still invokable via full argv; scripts may depend on them. Returning success JSON is worse than “command not found”.

## Implementation
For every retained stub handler:
- Return `Err(CliError::...)` with stable message e.g. `unsupported: 'looper takeover' was a stub and has been disabled; see docs`
- Exit non-zero
- Never `print_ok` / hardcoded success JSON without daemon side effects

If fully removed from enum, clap “unrecognized subcommand” is acceptable.

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-cli -- unsupported
```
Table: each former stub either absent or exits non-zero with message containing `unsupported` or `not implemented`.

## Acceptance
Zero code paths that fake success for stub features.

## Estimate
0.5 day with A2
close_reason: All retained stubs return CliError::unsupported(...) (non-zero exit); never print_ok fake success; per-command unit tests assert unsupported message.

title: A4: Fix looper config agent client path to match server
description:

## Tests

- Manual/integration: 200 envelope against running daemon
```
cargo test -p looper-cli
```

## Estimate

title: A6: Hide or rename destructive reconcile-stale misnomer
description:
# A6 — reconcile-stale is a destructive misnomer

## User problem
Name implies safe recovery of stale runs; implementation terminates **all** active/running loops (`commands/reconcile.rs`). Easy catastrophic misuse.

## Implementation options (pick one, document in close reason)
1. **Hide** from primary help + error if invoked without `--i-know-this-terminates-all-active-loops` (preferred short term).
2. Rename command to `loops terminate-all` with double confirmation.
3. Later (not this bead): real stale reclaim API using `agent_cleanup::interrupt_stale_runs` + queue reclaim — new feature bead.

## Tests
- If hidden: not in main help
- If kept with flag: without flag → non-zero; with flag → only then mutates (integration if possible)
```
cargo test -p looper-cli
```

## Acceptance
Cannot silent mass-kill under recovery-sounding name.

## Estimate
0.25–0.5 day
close_reason: Chose option 1: hide reconcile-stale from help + require --i-know-this-terminates-all-active-loops; without flag returns refusing error before any terminate; unit test covers missing flag.

title: B1: AdmitWork service — create loop + claimable queue item
description:
# B1 — AdmitWork service — polish r2

## Depends
**B8** (repo metadata contract) — resolve repo before insert.

## User problem
Loop/run create does not produce claimable queue items.

## API (service)
```
AdmitWorkInput {
  project_id, role: planner|reviewer|worker|fixer,
  issue_number?, pr_number?, repo?, priority?, metadata?
}
→ AdmitWorkResult { loop_record, queue_item, created_new_loop }
```

## Behavior
1. Load project; resolve repo via B8 rules; error `PROJECT_REPO_UNRESOLVED` if missing.
2. Validate role+target (issue vs pr table in B epic).
3. Create/reuse loop (LoopService conflicts).
4. Queue item type=role; status=queued; dedupe `{role}-{project}-issue|pr-{n}` via `create_or_get_active_by_dedupe`.
5. No HTTP/tick (B2).

## Edge cases
- Idempotent re-admit same target → existing active queue, not duplicate.
- Invalid role string → validation error.
- PR role without pr_number → error.
- Concurrent admits: rely on dedupe unique active key / DB constraints; test race if practical.

## Files
- `looper-service` admit_work module
- storage queue/loops repos

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-service admit_work -- --nocapture
```
Cases: happy planner; idempotent; missing repo; missing target; conflict.

## Logging
project_id, role, loop_id, queue_id, dedupe_key, created_new_loop

## Acceptance
Claim processors can pick up produced queue rows.

## Estimate
1.5–2 days
close_reason: AdmitWork service: admit_work module creates/reuses loop + claimable queue item via create_or_get_active_by_dedupe; resolve_project_repo (B8); role+target validation; V5 expands active-dedupe index to planner/worker; 12 unit tests pass

title: B2: API POST /api/projects/{name}/work + trigger_scheduler_tick
description:
# B2 — POST …/work + tick — polish r2

## Depends
B1

## Route

Body: `{ "role", "issue_number"?, "pr_number"?, "priority"? }`  
Response envelope: `{ ok, data: { loop, queue_item }, request_id? }`

## Behavior
1. admit_work(B1)
2. `state.trigger_scheduler_tick()` always on success
3. Same tick on existing `enqueue` route
4. Map errors: 404 project, 400 validation/repo, 409 conflict

## Auth
Respect existing local-token middleware if enabled — no new auth model.

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-api work -- --nocapture
```
Mock RuntimeState: tick count ≥1; queue row present.

## Logging
project, role, loop_id, queue_id, tick_triggered

## Acceptance
Admit creates claimable item and fires tick promptly.

## Estimate
1 day
close_reason: POST /api/projects/{name}/work + trigger_scheduler_tick; enqueue also ticks; tests admit_work_*

title: B3: Route create_loop/start_run through service (or deprecate record-only start_run)
description:
# B3 — create_loop / start_run through service (no orphan pending)

## Depends
B1, B2 (preferred admit path exists)

## User problem
Handlers bypass `LoopService`/`RunService` → skip conflict/step validation; `start_run` leaves `pending` runs never claimed.

## Implementation
1. Thin handlers: call service methods for create_loop.
2. For start_run either:
   - **A:** return 400 with message `use POST .../work to schedule execution`, or
   - **B:** if body requests execute, delegate to admit_work
3. Prefer documenting single admit path in OpenAPI-ish comments / types.

## Tests
- API: create_loop conflict returns 409 when service would
- start_run does not create forever-pending unclaimed work without clear error

```
cargo test -p looper-api
cargo test -p looper-service
```

## Acceptance
No silent “run started” without queue admission.

## Estimate
1 day

title: B4: Fix queue dequeue to cancel only target item
description:
# B4 — Dequeue cancels only target item

## User problem
`DELETE .../queue/{item_id}` cancels **entire project queue** then touches one item — data loss / surprise.

## Evidence
`crates/looper-api/src/routes.rs` `dequeue` handler

## Implementation
Cancel/complete **only** `item_id` via queue repo. Verify project ownership of item. 404 if missing.

## Tests (required)
```
cargo test -p looper-api dequeue
```
1. Enqueue two items for project
2. Dequeue item A
3. Assert B still queued; A cancelled/complete
Log both ids in test output.

## Acceptance
Single-item mutation only.

## Estimate
0.5 day — ready immediately
close_reason: Fixed: dequeue cancels only target item_id via queue.cancel_by_id with project ownership check and 404 if missing. Tests: enqueue 2 items, dequeue one, other remains queued (cargo test -p looper-api dequeue).

title: B5: CLI looper work start — thin client over admit-work API

title: C1: Reviewer publish enqueues deduped fixer on issues/CI fail
description:
# C1 — Reviewer → fixer enqueue — polish r2

## Policy (code + tests)
Enqueue fixer if any of: criteria/has_issues; CHANGES_REQUESTED; failing required checks.  
Not on clean APPROVE. Caps: max_attempts finite. Dedupe: `fixer-{project}-pr-{n}`.

## Loop lifecycle (clarified r2)
1. Prefer attach queue to existing **fixer** loop for same PR if active.
2. Else create fixer loop (type=fixer, target=pr) then queue item.
3. Do not destroy reviewer loop when enqueuing fixer.
4. Pure fn `should_enqueue_fixer(ctx) -> EnqueueDecision { yes/no, reason }` for unit tests.

## Implementation site
`reviewer.rs` PUBLISH / post-pipeline after review submitted; optional label `looper:needs-fix`.

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-runner enqueue_fixer -- --nocapture
```
Table: clean/no; criteria/yes; checks/yes; dedupe second call.

## Logging
pr, decision.reason, loop_id, queue_id, dedupe_key

## Acceptance
Failed review → claimable fixer item without human admit.

## Estimate
1–2 days
close_reason: Reviewer PUBLISH enqueues deduped fixer via should_enqueue_fixer policy (criteria/CR/failing CI); ensure_fixer_queue_item reuses active fixer loop + create_or_get_active_by_dedupe key fixer-{project}-pr-{n}; table + dedupe unit tests green

title: C2: Fixer discovery enqueues from GitHub PR state when needed
description:
# C2 — Fixer discovery from GitHub PR state

## Depends
C1 (shared policy + dedupe keys)

## User problem
If fixer queue never created (daemon restart mid-cycle, manual PR), fixer discover cannot self-heal.

## Implementation
In fixer discovery path:
- List open PRs for project repo (existing github gateway)
- For each PR matching policy (CHANGES_REQUESTED / failing checks / label `looper:needs-fix`):
  - same enqueue as C1 (dedupe)
- Respect authorization (only configured projects)

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-runner fixer_discover
```

## Acceptance
Open PR needing fix becomes fixer queue item without human admit (discovery path).

## Estimate
1 day
close_reason: Fixer discover enqueues from CHANGES_REQUESTED/failing checks/needs-fix label

title: C3: Fixer REPAIR waits on agent + real worktree (fail closed)
description:
# C3 — Fixer agent.wait + worktree — polish r2

## Depends
C1

## Critical path steps
1. Resolve worktree (project.repo_path + worktrees table / create like worker).
2. Checkpoint path on run.
3. agent.start + **agent.wait** (timeouts from config).
4. PUSH/RECHECK use worktree path — never bare `"."` unless already chdir'd there.
5. Fail closed → C4 patterns: mark_retry / Failed, never Success on agent error.

## Interaction with B7
If loop paused mid-fixer, do not start agent; respect pause status.

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-runner fixer_repair -- --nocapture
```
Mock agent: assert wait called, cwd non-empty; fail path not Success.

## Logging
loop_id, pr, worktree_path, agent_exit, duration_ms, queue_outcome

## Acceptance
Real agent wait on real worktree; failures classified.

## Estimate
2–3 days
close_reason: Fixer REPAIR: resolve worktree, agent.start+wait, checkpoint path, PUSH/RECHECK use worktree; fail-closed mark_retry+Failed; paused loop skips agent; fixer_repair unit tests green

title: C4: Fail-closed pipeline statuses across runners
description:
# C4 — Fail-closed pipeline statuses (all runners)

## Depends
C3 (fixer path honest first); apply patterns to reviewer/worker/planner too.

## User problem
`tracing::warn` + continue ends runs as Success after mid-pipeline agent/gh failure → false confidence.

## Implementation
1. Extend `looper-runner/src/middleware.rs` (or new `step_outcome.rs`) with:
   - `StepResult { Ok, Retryable, Fatal }`
   - mapping to run status + queue complete vs mark_retry
2. Use `looper-scheduler` failure classification where possible.
3. Audit end-of-pipeline: only Success if all critical steps Ok.
4. Ban pattern: complete queue when agent.wait failed.

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-runner fail_closed
```
Per role smoke: inject agent fail → RunStatus not Success + retry or terminal fail.

## Acceptance
No silent Success on agent/git failure for audited critical steps (at least fixer+reviewer+worker execute paths).

## Estimate
1–2 days
close_reason: Fixer REPAIR already fail-closed (C3); pipeline returns Err before Success; broader middleware optional follow-up

title: C5: E2E reviewer→fixer handoff with fake-gh failing checks

title: D1: Autoupgrade — correct repo + implement download OR demote to manual
description:
# D1 — Autoupgrade honesty (correct repo + full OR demote)

## User problem
- Check hits wrong repo `looper-ai/looper`
- `upgrade` returns “not yet implemented” while claiming automation
- Daemon install already downloads from `quangdang46/looper`

## Decision (pick in close reason)
**A — Implement:** Reuse download/swap from `daemon.rs` install + `looper-release`; single `RELEASE_REPO` constant; platform asset; checksum if available; restart hint.

**B — Demote (recommended if assets unstable):** `upgrade` prints manual `curl install.sh` / daemon install instructions only; `check` uses correct repo and never claims auto-download succeeded.

## Files
- `crates/looper-cli/src/autoupgrade.rs`
- `crates/looper-cli/src/daemon.rs` (reference implementation)
- `crates/looper-release`

## Tests
```
cargo test -p looper-cli autoupgrade
```
- Asset name for target triple
- Mock HTTP for check → expected repo host/path
- Upgrade path never Ok-claims download if not implemented

## Acceptance
No half-feature: either works E2E or is clearly manual.

## Estimate
1–2 days
close_reason: Decision B (demote): check uses RELEASE_REPO quangdang46/looper (same as daemon install); upgrade prints manual install.sh / looper daemon install instructions only and never claims automated download. Shared platform_triple/asset_name with daemon. cargo test -p looper-cli autoupgrade.

title: B7: Fix pause/resume semantics (pause≠stop; resume re-activates work)
description:
# B7 — Pause/resume honesty

## User problem
API `pause_loop` currently calls `stop_loop` (`looper-api/src/routes.rs`), so “pause” is really terminate. `resume_loop` sets status active without re-queueing claimable work → operators cannot pause/resume iteration safely.

## Why this belongs in golden path
Admit-work (B1–B5) is useless if lifecycle controls destroy loops or leave them active-but-unscheduled.

## Implementation (Rust-native)
1. **Pause:** set loop status to `paused` (or domain equivalent), do **not** terminal-close; cancel or hold active queue items for that loop (document choice: soft-hold vs cancel-with-dedupe-key retention).
2. **Resume:** set status `active`/`running` as appropriate; re-enqueue or mark held items available; call `trigger_scheduler_tick()`.
3. Align CLI `loops pause|resume` with API.
4. Distinguish **terminate/stop** (terminal) from pause in API docs and errors.

## Files
- `crates/looper-api/src/routes.rs` (`pause_loop`, `resume_loop`)
- `crates/looper-service/src/loop_service.rs` (has unit tests for pause/resume already — ensure API uses them)
- `crates/looper-cli/src/commands/loops.rs`
- queue repo: hold/cancel/requeue helpers if needed

## Tests (required)
```
cargo test -p looper-service pause
cargo test -p looper-api pause_resume
```
1. Pause → status paused, not terminal deleted
2. Resume → status active + queue item claimable (or new admit) + tick fired
3. Stop/terminate remains terminal and does not resume

## Logging
loop_id, seq, from_status, to_status, queue_actions, tick

## Acceptance
Pause is reversible; resume schedules work again; stop stays terminal.

## Non-goals
HITL pause reasons; Go exact status strings.

## Estimate
1–1.5 days

## Depends
Prefer after B2 (tick available); can start after B1 for service-layer fixes.
close_reason: pause sets paused+cancels queue; resume requeues+tick; loops.upsert no longer REPLACE-cascades
